name: che-word-mcp
description: v3.17.5 ooxml-swift dep bump 0.21.8 → 0.21.9 — triple #87 + #93 + #94 release. #87 (Comment.paragraphIndex flat-counter, observable behavior change): list_comments paragraph_index now consistently 0-indexed against get_paragraphs() flat list; pre-fix off-by-N for any docx with non-paragraph BodyChild siblings before commented paragraph; callers manually compensating with paragraph_index - 1 must remove compensation. #93 (wrap_caption_seq SEQ inherits source position, caption visual fix): pre-fix 「圖 4-1：xxx」 became 「圖 4-：xxx1」 because new SEQ run had position=nil while source-loaded preText/postText had position>0; Paragraph emit bifurcates by position so SEQ landed at end instead of spliced; one-line fix seqRun.position = preRun.position; insert_bookmark=true × source-loaded paragraph still has same gap on bookmarkStart/End markers (filed PsychQuant/ooxml-swift#24, default insert_bookmark=false unaffected). #94 (update_all_fields traverses .table and .contentControl containers): pre-fix body loop only processed top-level .paragraph BodyChild, silently skipped .table and .contentControl(_, children:) — SEQ fields inside table cells/block-level SDTs never updated, returning 'no SEQ fields found' for thesis docs (caption paragraphs commonly live inside the table they describe); same gap #68 closed for findBodyChildContainingText; new walkAndProcessBodyChildForFields recursive walker mirrors #68 pattern; heading-count semantics: only top-level direct .paragraph body children count toward chapter-reset (container-nested headings do NOT trigger SEQ resets). Known incompleteness (3 follow-ups filed): ooxml-swift#25 header/footer/footnote/endnote SEQ scans still flat .paragraphs view; ooxml-swift#26 FieldParser.parse(paragraph:) misses inline SDT/hyperlink/fieldSimple/alternateContent surfaces; ooxml-swift#27 verify-with-user-fixture for real thesis docx roundtrip; plus ooxml-swift#28 refactor candidate (extract BodyChildVisitor protocol to dedupe 5+ body-children walkers). Verified by 6-AI ensemble (5 Claude reviewers + Codex gpt-5.5 xhigh). Tests: 236 passing che-word-mcp / 805→809 ooxml-swift. Backward compatible except #87 documented behavior change. v3.17.4 ooxml-swift dep bump 0.21.7 → 0.21.8 — paired #91 + #92 release. #91 (insertEquation inline-mode error semantics): InsertLocationError.inlineModeRequiresParagraphIndex (anchor type other than .paragraphIndex with inline math) + .invalidParagraphIndex(Int) (negative or ≥ topLevelParagraphCount). MCP impact: insert_equation paragraph_index out-of-bounds now returns structured error instead of silent fallthrough. #92 (flattenedDisplayText OMML wrapper coverage): extracted flattenRunsWithOMML helper; applied to top-level runs + hyperlinks + fieldSimples + AC.fallbackRuns. MCP impact: anchor lookups (insert_paragraph / insert_image_from_path / insert_caption / replace_text before_text/after_text) against paragraphs containing inline math inside hyperlink/fldSimple/AC fallback wrappers now succeed where they previously silently 0-matched. Strict superset of pre-fix behavior. Tests: 236 passing (no new MCP tests, no regressions, 9 pre-existing skips). Backward compatible. Follow-ups #99-#103 filed in che-word-mcp tracker for sibling bugs (DA-1..DA-5: direct-child OMML in <w:p> / <w:hyperlink> / <mc:Fallback> + nested wrapper + docstring qualifier). v3.17.3 ooxml-swift dep bump 0.21.6 → 0.21.7 — pure transitive bump exposing PsychQuant/ooxml-swift#86 public anchor lookup API. WordDocument.findBodyChildContainingText(_:nthInstance:) + bodyChildContainsText (static) + tableContainsText (static) are now public, eliminating fork-and-diverge for external Swift SPM consumers (rescue scripts, dxedit CLI, third-party tooling). MCP layer can now de-duplicate its own anchor-lookup helpers in a future cleanup. No MCP tool surface change. 236 tests passing, no regressions, backward compatible. v3.17.2 ooxml-swift dep bump 0.21.2 → 0.21.6 (pure transitive bump, no MCP source changes; 4 ooxml-swift releases worth of hardening + new APIs land transparently). v0.21.3 (XML hardening, PsychQuant/ooxml-swift#7): DTD reject + 64KB attribute-value cap + SAX-based root-element attribute parsing + attribute-name whitelist on emit. New XMLHardeningError throws on DTD-bearing input, oversized attribute values, or invalid attribute names — surfaces malicious .docx as typed errors instead of silent acceptance. v0.21.4 (roundtrip loud-fail, PsychQuant/ooxml-swift#6): AlternateContent.fallbackRunsModified dirty flag throws RoundtripError.unserializedFallbackEdit when in-memory fallbackRuns mutation isn't propagated back to rawXML — closes the #56 silent UX failure where replace_text reported success but disk write was stale. Run.commentIds @available(*, deprecated); migrate to commentRangeMarkers (single source of truth). v0.21.5 (insertEquation flexibility, PsychQuant/che-word-mcp#84 #85): InsertLocation overload for Document.insertEquation + flattenedDisplayText OMML coverage extends anchor lookup to include equation visible text — caller's anchor-text resolution now traverses display equations. v0.21.6 (mutation surface fix, PsychQuant/ooxml-swift#5): Hyperlink.text setter @available(*, deprecated, message: 'Mutates runs destructively'); migrate to .runs property to preserve formatting. Position field cascade Int = 0 → Int? = nil across 13 typed-child models (Hyperlink, Run, AlternateContent, FieldSimple, Field, ParagraphChildMarkers + subtypes) with tri-state semantics (nil = unset, 0 = legacy front-pinned, > 0 = explicit ordering); 27 Paragraph.swift sites bulk-transformed via (position ?? 0) > 0 patterns to preserve backward compatibility. xml:space=preserve autosense in Run.toXMLThrowing — fires only when text starts/ends with whitespace OR contains consecutive whitespace runs (avoids attribute bloat). Plus 3 unreleased docs commits on ooxml-swift main (PsychQuant/ooxml-swift#14 #15 #17 + corrective cd841e7) covering needsPPr emit-gate ↔ Issue4 lock-in test bidirectional reference, parseRun vs parseParagraph walker pattern divergence rationale, foreign-namespace pPr asymmetry documentation. Tests: 236 passing (no new tests, no regressions, 9 pre-existing skips). Backward compatible: deprecation warnings only for Hyperlink.text setter and commentIds; no API removals. v3.17.1 ooxml-swift dep bump 0.21.0 → 0.21.2 (pPr regression guard + test infrastructure hardening from upstream PsychQuant/ooxml-swift#4 walker whitelist + #if DEBUG assert / PsychQuant/ooxml-swift#13 empty <w:pPr/> self-closing test gap / PsychQuant/ooxml-swift#16 countPPrOpenTags regex hardening — pure transitive bump). v3.17.0 wrap_caption_seq MCP tool (Refs #62): Phase 2 of cross-repo work — exposes ooxml-swift v0.21.0 lib API as MCP tool. Bulk-wraps plain-text caption number portions in SEQ field runs across body paragraphs whose flattened text matches a regex (EXACTLY ONE numeric capture group). Captured digit becomes SEQ field cachedResult so Word's first-open render preserves user-typed numbering before F9. Rescues docs pasted from external sources (LaTeX-converted Word, Google Docs, Pandoc) so insert_table_of_figures / insert_table_of_tables produce populated TOFs. Idempotent: paragraphs already wrapping a SEQ field for sequence_name reported in skipped, never double-wrapped (detection covers both FieldSimple AND rawXML fldChar emissions). Phase 1 ships scope:body only (recurses into table cells + nestedTables + block-level SDT children); scope:all returns Error: scope_not_implemented for now (cross-container path lands in v3.17.x). Bookmark wrap opt-in (insert_bookmark + bookmark_template with literal ${number}) so default 23-caption rescue does NOT pollute list_bookmarks. Returns JSON: {matched_paragraphs, fields_inserted, paragraphs_modified:[idx,...], skipped:[{paragraph_index, reason},...]}. All preconditions checked BEFORE document mutation (regex compile + capture-group count + format/scope enums + bookmark_template invariant + doc_id opened). Tests: 5 new sub-tests in Issue62WrapCaptionSeqTests covering Scenarios 1-5. Suite 231 → 236 (+5, 0 fail / 9 skip). No ooxml-swift dep bump (still v0.21.0 from v3.16.2). v3.16.2 ooxml-swift dep bump 0.20.5 → 0.21.0 (Refs #62 #68): pure dep bump, no MCP source changes. Picks up two ooxml-swift fixes that surface transparently via existing tool dispatch. #68 (ooxml-swift v0.20.6): InsertLocation.findBodyChildContainingText now traverses .table (rows × cells × paragraphs + nestedTables) and .contentControl(_, children:) (recursive). MCP impact — insert_paragraph / insert_image_from_path / insert_equation / insert_caption calls using before_text / after_text now succeed when anchor text lives inside a table cell or block-level SDT (common in thesis docs with figure/table captions inside table cells). Returned position is top-level body.children index of the containing structure. Use into_table_cell for inside-cell inserts. Empty-needle guard: passing before_text:'' / after_text:'' now returns textNotFound instead of silently inserting at index 1. #62 (ooxml-swift v0.21.0): WordDocument.wrapCaptionSequenceFields(...) is now linked into the binary. Not yet exposed as an MCP tool — the wrap_caption_seq MCP wrapper ships in v3.17.0 (Phase 2 of the cross-repo work). Existing MCP tools unaffected. Suite: 231 → 231 (0 fail / 9 skip). v3.16.1 anchorPresence whitelist drift prevention (Refs #80): pure refactor, no runtime behavior change. New static toolAnchorWhitelists dict (single source of truth, keyed by MCP tool name → accepted anchor list) + new detectPresentAnchors(_:tool:) overload. 4 conflict-detection call sites switched from literal anchor arrays to (tool:) lookup. 4 new invariant/parity tests. Suite 227 → 231 (+4). Old (args, anchors:) overload preserved. Out-of-scope follow-ups: schema descriptions + dispatcher if-else chains still hardcode anchor names (pre-existing surfaces, not introduced by this PR). v3.16.0 Bundle B anchor DX consistency (Refs #70 #71 #72): BREAKING (input validation only) — three coordinated MCP-layer changes across the 4 #61-target tools. #71 (behavior) silent priority on conflicting anchors → structured error: insert_paragraph(after_text + index) was previously silent-priority; now returns 'Error: insert_paragraph: received conflicting anchors: after_text + index. Specify exactly one.' New static helper detectPresentAnchors with per-anchor type-aware predicates (null and wrong-type values do NOT count). #72 (validation) explicit text_instance ≤ 0 rejected — 'Error: <tool>: text_instance must be ≥ 1, got <N>.' Omitted text_instance still defaults to 1. #70 (DX) all 32 'return Error:' lines in 4 #61-target tools rewritten as 'Error: <tool>: <body>' for AI-caller error attribution. throw WordError.* paths unchanged. Scope deliberately limited to 4 tools; remaining 41 return Error lines elsewhere deferred to error-prefix-sweep follow-up. SemVer rationale (minor not major): no schema break, no tool removal, restricting previously-undefined behavior. Tests: 201 → 227 (+26 sub-tests, 0 fail / 9 skip). No ooxml-swift dep bump (still v0.20.5). v3.15.3 Bundle A2 polish from v3.15.2 verify R3-R6 follow-ups (Refs #76 #77 #78 #79): #76 (docs) insert_caption description corrected from '三種 anchor' to enumerate all 5 (paragraph_index / after_image_id / after_table_index / after_text / before_text); insert_equation paragraph_index description clarified that the int is body.children-indexed (cross-references PsychQuant/ooxml-swift#10 for the lib-layer convention split). #77 (docs) insert_caption anchor set wording precision in CHANGELOG / manifest / marketplace.json / plugin.json — was 'its own anchor set including after_table_index' (implies disjoint), now 'shares after_image_id / after_text / before_text / paragraph_index, adds after_table_index + position, lacks into_table_cell' (explicit shared/adds/lacks). #78 (test) extends #69 append-index regression pin to bookmarkMarker / rawBlockElement / block-level contentControl body-children — the table case alone wouldn't catch a regression to getParagraphs().count - 1 that breaks for SDT / TOC bookmark / vendor extensions. #79 (test) adds round-trip depth: testInsertParagraphAppendIndexRoundTripsForInsertCalls demonstrates insert-family round-trip works (append + insert(N+1) + verify ordering); testInsertParagraphAppendIndexCannotRoundTripToUpdate pins the cross-family trade-off (update_paragraph(index=N) throws WordError.invalidIndex). Tests: 196 → 201 (+5 sub-tests, 0 fail / 9 skip). No production code change. No ooxml-swift dep bump (still v0.20.5). v3.15.2 closes Bundle A polish from #61 R2 verify (Refs #69 #73 #74 #75): #69 (bug) insert_paragraph append message reports body.children index instead of getParagraphs().count - 1 (mis-reported in docs with tables/SDTs by skipping table children); #74 (bug) insert_image_from_path debug log labels after_image_id correctly (was silently labeled 'index'); #73 (test) regression pin for equation F5 partial-dict guard (existed since v3.15.1 but was untested); #75 (docs) clarifies '3 insert tools' wording — scope is the 3 #61-target tools (insert_paragraph / insert_equation / insert_image_from_path); insert_caption is a 4th insert tool with a partially-overlapping anchor set (shares after_image_id / after_text / before_text / paragraph_index, adds after_table_index + position, lacks into_table_cell), intentionally outside this unification scope. Tests: 194 → 196 (0 fail / 9 skip). No behavior change in normal call paths. No ooxml-swift dep bump (still v0.20.5). Swift-native MCP server for Microsoft Word document (.docx) manipulation. v3.15.1 closes verify findings F1+F2+F3+F5 from v3.15.0 6-AI ensemble (5 Claude reviewers + Codex gpt-5.5 xhigh): F1 (P1) after_image_id anchor missing on insert_paragraph + insert_equation + insert_image_from_path — lib InsertLocation.afterImageId existed since #44 but only insert_caption was wired; F2 (P1) into_table_cell missing on insert_equation — display mode equation is structurally a new paragraph; F3 (P2) equation success message lacked anchor info — same v3.14.4 LOOKUP-over-claim pattern (now mirrors paragraph: 'Inserted equation (display mode: true, after text X (instance N))'); F5 (P2) malformed into_table_cell partial dict (e.g. {table_index: 0} missing row+col) silently fell through to next anchor / append — now returns structured 'Error: into_table_cell requires all three fields' in all 3 tools. Anchor priority unified across all 3 #61-target tools (insert_paragraph / insert_equation / insert_image_from_path): into_table_cell > after_image_id > after_text > before_text > index > append. Inline equation rejects all anchor params with structured error (anchor semantics ambiguous; use paragraph_index). Tests: Issue61V315PointReleaseTests (9 sub-tests covering F1×3 tools / F2 display+inline / F3 message format / F5 in 2 tools). Suite 185 → 194 (0 fail / 9 pre-existing skips). No ooxml-swift dep bump (still v0.20.5). Follow-up issues for F4 (inline equation more general design) / F6 (text anchor traversal into tables/SDTs) / F7 (getParagraphs index mismatch — pre-existing) / F8 (error tool-prefix) / F9 (conflicting anchor warnings) / F10 (text_instance<=0 normalization). v3.15.0 closes #61 — insert_paragraph and insert_equation now accept the same anchor parameters as insert_image_from_path: after_text / before_text / text_instance / into_table_cell (insert_paragraph also). Pre-fix MCP layer silently dropped these arguments — JSON schema accepted them but handler dispatch ignored them, so calls fell through to legacy paragraph_index path or appended at end. Lib API Document.insertParagraph(_: at: InsertLocation) has supported all six anchor cases since #44; this release closes the MCP-side wire-up gap. Anchor priority mirrors insert_image_from_path: into_table_cell > after_text > before_text > index > append. Errors (textNotFound / tableIndexOutOfRange / tableCellOutOfRange) return structured messages instead of silent fallthrough. insert_equation in inline mode (display_mode=false) explicitly rejects anchors with structured error — semantics ambiguous between 'append OMML run into existing para' vs 'insert new para before/after target'; use paragraph_index for inline placement. Tests: Issue61InsertParagraphAnchorsSmokeTests (5 sub-tests covering after_text / before_text / text_instance disambiguation / into_table_cell / textNotFound error) + Issue61InsertEquationAnchorsSmokeTests (4 sub-tests covering after_text + before_text in display mode / inline mode rejection / textNotFound error). Suite 176 → 185 (0 fail). No ooxml-swift dep bump (v0.20.5 has all needed lib APIs from #44). Backward compatible — all anchor parameters optional; existing index / paragraph_index callers unchanged. v3.14.5 closes Refs #63 verify F1 P1: extends findBodyChildContainingText (used by InsertLocation.afterText / .beforeText resolution) to mirror Document.replaceInParagraphSurfaces surface coverage. Pre-fix v3.14.4 only fixed REPLACE path (replace_text); LOOKUP path (insert_image_from_path / insert_paragraph / insert_caption before_text/after_text) still threw textNotFound against SDT-wrapped anchors despite v3.14.4 CHANGELOG claim. ooxml-swift v0.20.5 adds TextReplacementEngine.flatTextOfContentXML (read-only XML walker mirroring replaceInContentXML flattening rules) + Paragraph.flattenedDisplayText extension covering runs + hyperlinks + fieldSimples + alternateContents + contentControls (recursive into nested SDT). findBodyChildContainingText now uses flattenedDisplayText. Suite 693 → 696 ooxml-swift / 174 → 176 che-word-mcp. Bumps ooxml-swift v0.20.4 → v0.20.5. Backward compatible — strict superset of pre-fix lookup behavior. v3.14.4 fixes replace_text on inline <w:sdt> content controls (closes #63): Document.replaceInParagraphSurfaces previously covered paragraph.runs / hyperlinks / fieldSimples / alternateContents but not paragraph.contentControls. Text wrapped in inline SDT (common in docx output from external converters: pandoc / Quarto / LaTeX→docx wrap cross-ref placeholders [tab:foo] / [fig:bar] / [Smith 2020] in inline SDTs by convention) was silently 0-matched. Issue title 'literal [ ] brackets' was misleading — brackets are coincidence; bracket-free text inside inline SDTs failed too. Fix targets wrapper coverage gap, not bracket-as-regex-char escaping. ooxml-swift v0.20.4 adds TextReplacementEngine.replaceInContentXML (XML DOM walker that flattens <w:t> descendants of ContentControl.content with offset map and splices replacements back into <w:t> string content) + Document.replaceInContentControl (recursive helper for nested SDT children). Skipped during the walk: <w:delText> (TC deletion text), <w:instrText> (field instruction code), nested <w:sdt> subtrees (typed cc.children handled by outer recursion). Round-trip discipline: only <w:t> element string content is mutated; xml:space=preserve and other attributes survive verbatim. Differential test Issue63InlineSDTReplaceTests covers 4 wrappers (fldChar / fldSimple / hyperlink / inlineSDT) × 4 needles + nested SDT recursion + round-trip preservation. Suite: 690 → 693 ooxml-swift, 172 → 174 che-word-mcp. Bumps ooxml-swift v0.20.3 → v0.20.4. Backward compatible — surgical fix adds new code paths only. v3.14.3 sub-stack E of paragraph-level content-equality (closes #66): Paragraph.w14ParaId / w14TextId extract and round-trip the w14:* attributes on <w:p> opening tag — Word's revision-tracking GUIDs anchoring paragraph identity for collaborative editing and comment threading. Plain attribute passthrough with String? typing (Word's GUIDs are 8-char hex tokens, NOT RFC 4122 UUIDs). Pre-fix v3.14.2 silently dropped both attributes — accounted for ~95% of w14:* token loss in NTPU thesis fixture (2214 of 2359 lost tokens). Post-E measured impact: w14: retention 10.55% → 93.98%; document.xml size loss 10.95% → 8.02%. Combined with sub-stack D (#65), total impact since v3.14.1: <w:lang> 50% → 98.89% (D), w14:* 5% → 93.98% (E), document.xml size loss 16.66% → 8.02% (D+E, -8.64 pp). Matrix-pin testDocumentContentEqualityInvariant ratchets w14: floor 0.04 → 0.90; sizeLossRatio ceiling 0.12 → 0.10. The matrix-pin is now LOAD-BEARING across 5 preservation classes (rFonts / noProof / lang / kern / w14:) spanning run-level + paragraph-level + paragraph-mark scope. Defensive design (R2 review fixes): openingPTag() routes attributes through escapeXMLAttribute; parseParagraph rejects schema-invalid empty-string GUIDs. Bumps ooxml-swift v0.20.2 → v0.20.3. Backward compatible (both fields optional, default nil; openingPTag empty-attrs gate prevents synthetic emit). Suite: 686 → 690 pass / 0 fail / 1 skip. Architecture context: completes 'che-word-mcp-paragraph-level-content-equality' Spectra change (sub-stacks D + E). Remaining 8% loss is dominated by other w14:* attribute classes (e.g., w14:* on <w:r>) — tracked as separate follow-up SDD. v3.14.2 sub-stack D of paragraph-level content-equality (closes #65): ParagraphProperties.markRunProperties extracts and round-trips <w:rPr> direct child of <w:pPr> (paragraph-mark formatting per ECMA-376 §17.3.1.27 CT_PPrBase) — controls pilcrow ¶ glyph appearance. Reuses parseRunProperties verbatim — schema is identical to run-level CT_RPr, so all of sub-stack C's typed extraction (rFonts 4-axis, noProof, kern, lang 3-axis) and rawChildren passthrough (w14:* effects) come for free with zero schema duplication. Measured impact on NTPU thesis fixture: <w:lang> retention 50% → 98.89%, <w:rFonts> 88% → 98.77%, <w:noProof> 92% → 100%, <w:kern> 84% → 99.93%, document.xml size loss 16.66% → 10.95%. Matrix-pin testDocumentContentEqualityInvariant ratchets in lockstep (lang 0.45→0.95, rFonts/noProof/kern 0.95, sizeLossRatio ceiling 0.175→0.12). Sub-stack E (#66 w14:paraId/textId on <w:p>) ships next, completing the path to <5% round-trip loss. Bumps ooxml-swift v0.20.1→v0.20.2. Backward compatible (markRunProperties optional, default nil). v3.14.1 sub-stack C-CONT closes triple-confirmed P0 (R2 + R5 + Codex 6-AI verify): recognizedRprChildren Set listed ~16+ rPr child kinds as 'recognized' but parseRunProperties had NO extraction for them → silent drop on read. Affected: <w:spacing> (character spacing), <w:caps>/<w:smallCaps>, <w:position>, <w:shd> (run shading), <w:bdr>, <w:em> (CJK emphasis marks), <w:effect>, <w:vanish>/<w:specVanish>/<w:webHidden>, <w:outline>/<w:shadow>/<w:emboss>/<w:imprint>, <w:bCs>/<w:iCs>/<w:dstrike> — all very common in real Word docs. Fix: trim Set to ONLY actually-typed-extracted-or-emitted kinds. Everything else falls into rawChildren and round-trips byte-equivalent. Round-trip size loss: pre-fix v0.19.x 32% → v0.20.0 17.75% → v0.20.1 16.66%. Methodology lesson (6th): P2 from one reviewer can become P0 when another applies real-world impact lens. v3.14.0 closes #60 (sub-stack C of #58/#59/#60) — RunProperties field-loss audit. Adds typed fields: 4-axis rFonts (ascii/hAnsi/eastAsia/cs/hint — was collapsed to single value), noProof, kern, 3-axis lang (val/eastAsia/bidi), plus rawChildren passthrough for unrecognized rPr children (e.g., w14:textOutline / w14:textFill / w14:glow). Pre-fix MCP users saw eastAsia/cs fonts (e.g., DFKai-SB for traditional Chinese) silently replaced with ascii values on round-trip; v3.14.0 preserves all 4 axes independently. Matrix-pin testDocumentContentEqualityInvariant gains preservation-class-3 ratio-floor assertions making it LOAD-BEARING for any future RunProperties regression. Round-trip size loss for thesis fixture document.xml drops from pre-fix 32% to 17.75% (improvement of 14.25 percentage points); remaining 17.75% is paragraph-mark rPr + w14:paraId/textId drops (separate out-of-scope follow-up). Architectural completion of 'if not typed, preserve as raw' principle that started in sub-stack A (#58 BodyChild) and B (#59 WhitespaceOverlay). Backward compatible — legacy fontName field retained, mirrors rFonts.ascii. v3.13.13 CRITICAL HOTFIX (sub-stack B-CONT-2-CONT) reverted v3.13.12's TIER-0 over-fix that broke <w:del> round-trip. v3.13.12 (DO NOT USE — stripped <w:del> content). v3.13.11 sub-stack B-CONT (boundary check + 7 raw-capture sites + delText overlay + entity-decode + comments-trim removal). v3.13.10 sub-stack B initial WhitespaceOverlay implementation. v3.13.11: bumps ooxml-swift to v0.19.11 — sub-stack B-CONT (boundary check + 7 raw-capture sites + delText overlay + entity-decode + comments-trim removal). v3.13.10: bumps ooxml-swift to v0.19.10 — sub-stack B initial WhitespaceOverlay implementation (covers all 6 <w:t>-bearing parts). v3.13.10: bumps ooxml-swift to v0.19.10 — sub-stack B of #58/#59/#60 closes #59 initial WhitespaceOverlay implementation (covers all 6 <w:t>-bearing parts). v3.13.9: bumps ooxml-swift to v0.19.9 — A-CONT-3 mini-cycle of #58 closes 3 P0 from sub-stack A-CONT-2 6-AI verify INCLUDING SILENT CORRECTNESS REGRESSION (delete_bookmark for header/footer bookmarks was silently lost on disk in v3.13.8 — must-upgrade). Plus list_bookmarks now surfaces paragraph-level container bookmarks; insert_bookmark detects duplicate names across all 5 part types. Sub-cycle 4 for #58 (A → A-CONT → A-CONT-2 → A-CONT-3). Same convergence-cycle pattern as R5→R5-CONT-4 (5 sub-cycles for #56). v3.13.8: bumps ooxml-swift to v0.19.8 — A-CONT-2 mini-mini-cycle of #58 closes 2 P0 + 1 P1 from sub-stack A-CONT 6-AI verify (getBookmarks() now walks container bodyChildren across headers/footers/footnotes/endnotes; parseContainerChildBodyChildren now has 'case sdt' recursion mirroring parseBodyChildren; deleteBookmark() now symmetric with getBookmarks() — can delete names that getBookmarks() returns; matrix-pin synthetic fixture catches container parser regressions for real). Sub-cycle 3 for #58 — same convergence-cycle pattern as R5→R5-CONT-4 (5 sub-cycles for #56). v3.13.7: bumps ooxml-swift to v0.19.7 — A-CONT mini-cycle of #58 closes 2 P0 from sub-stack A 6-AI verify (parseContainerChildBodyChildren parser asymmetry: body-level bookmarks in headers/footers/footnotes/endnotes were still silently dropped post-v3.13.6; getBookmarks() walker missed body-level entries). Same convergence-cycle pattern as R5-CONT-4 (#56): per-task gate caught one parser entry point; 6-AI verify caught the symmetric sibling. v3.13.6: bumps ooxml-swift to v0.19.6 + word-to-md-swift to v0.6.1 — closes #58 (sub-stack A of #58/#59/#60 document-content-preservation): body-level <w:bookmarkStart>/<w:bookmarkEnd> (TOC _Toc<digits> anchors that wrap multiple paragraphs) now survive body-mutating save via BodyChild enum extension under 'if not typed, preserve as raw' principle. Cross-cutting matrix-pin testDocumentContentEqualityInvariant lands with bookmarkStart count parity (45=45 thesis fixture); B (#59 whitespace overlay) + C (#60 RunProperties audit) ship in subsequent v3.13.7/v3.14.0. v3.13.5: bumps ooxml-swift to v0.19.5 — closes R5 stack-completion (6 P0 + 5 P1 + R5-CONT 7 P0 + 5 P1 + R5-CONT-2 5 P0 + 4 P1 + R5-CONT-3 1 P0 + 4 P1 + R5-CONT-4 1 P0 + 3 P1 from #56 rounds 4-8 verify) via spectra-driven per-task verify gates (no che-word-mcp source changes). v0.19.4 was held back per verify-gate. Round 4 (R5 stack): walker symmetry across headers/footers/footnotes/endnotes — accept_revision / reject_revision / accept_all_revisions / reject_all_revisions / get_hyperlinks / replace_text now find wrappers in all parts. Round 5 (R5-CONT): per-container relationships round-trip — update_hyperlink URL sync targets owning part rels not document-scope. Round 6 (R5-CONT-2): delete_hyperlink mirror + reject_revision .insertion source-routing + container <w:tbl> capture preserved.
